metadata.description: Self-hosted best-practice plugins: UI/UX stacks, frameworks, databases, architecture, design patterns, testing, security, API design, dev environments, and the taskmaster clarification-to-execution workflow suite.

plugins.name: ui-ux
plugins.description: UI/UX best practices with per-stack skills: shadcn/ui, ReUI, Aceternity UI, Tailwind, CSS3, Bootstrap, CSS Grid, Flexbox — plus shadcn theme creation with a live colour-preview URL. Includes /ui-ux:review, /ui-ux:theme, and ui-ux-reviewer + ui-ux-engineer agents.

plugins.name: react
plugins.description: React best practices: hooks rules, render/memo performance, state management, component patterns. Includes /react:review.

plugins.name: react-native
plugins.description: React Native best practices: list performance, navigation, platform-specific code, animations. Includes /react-native:review.

plugins.name: vue2
plugins.description: Vue 2.7 best practices: Composition API backport, defineProperty reactivity caveats, Vue 3 migration readiness. Includes /vue2:review.

plugins.name: vue3
plugins.description: Vue 3 best practices: script setup, composables, ref/reactive pitfalls, Pinia. Includes /vue3:review.

plugins.name: php
plugins.description: PHP best practices: strict types, === discipline, PSR-4/PSR-12 conventions, version-aware advice pinned to the composer.json floor with a per-version leverage map (8.1-8.5), exceptions, DateTimeImmutable, boundary security, value objects, static analysis. Includes /php:review.

plugins.name: laravel
plugins.description: Laravel best practices: Eloquent N+1 prevention, form requests, service layer, queues, policies. Includes /laravel:review.

plugins.name: livewire
plugins.description: Livewire 3 best practices: component conventions, wire:model modifiers, performance, Alpine interop. Includes /livewire:review.

plugins.name: sql
plugins.description: Engine-agnostic SQL best practices: sargable predicates, join correctness, composite index logic, NULL traps, transactions, constraints, keyset pagination, additive migrations, parameterization. Includes /sql:review.

plugins.name: mysql
plugins.description: MySQL 8.0+ best practices: InnoDB clustered-PK design, utf8mb4, strict sql_mode, online DDL and metadata locks, gap locking, JSON limits, 8.0-8.4 LTS version leverage. Includes /mysql:review.

plugins.name: mariadb
plugins.description: MariaDB 10.6+ best practices: not-MySQL divergences, RETURNING, sequences, system-versioned tables, native UUID type, uca1400 collations, Galera awareness. Includes /mariadb:review.

plugins.name: postgresql
plugins.description: PostgreSQL 14+ best practices: MVCC/vacuum reality, timestamptz/jsonb/uuid types, partial/expression/GIN/BRIN indexes, lock-aware migrations, ON CONFLICT/RETURNING, version leverage through 17/18. Includes /postgresql:review.

plugins.name: code-architecture
plugins.description: Engineering process: plan-before-code with current-vs-target architecture diagrams on a live preview URL for structural changes, YAGNI checks, task orchestration, work verification, low-cognitive-load code, KISS/DRY simplicity principles, SOLID principles applied with judgment, and always-on surgical-coding discipline (surface assumptions, every changed line traces to the request, orphan rule, verifiable success criteria — after Karpathy's LLM-coding guidelines). Includes /code-architecture:plan|verify|yagni|solid and an architecture-reviewer agent. Owns code-level structure; defers system-level topology (service boundaries, scaling, caching) to the system-design plugin.

plugins.name: design-patterns
plugins.description: Design pattern selection: which pattern fits where, and when NOT to use one. Includes /design-patterns:suggest.

plugins.name: api-docs-first
plugins.description: Verify current API docs before writing integration code; asks for a URL or file when docs are not visible. Includes /api-docs-first:check and a UserPromptSubmit reminder hook. For consuming third-party APIs; defers designing your own REST APIs to api-design and Meta/Facebook platform specifics to meta-api.

plugins.name: meta-api
plugins.description: Meta (Facebook) developer platform navigator: always-current Graph API version from the changelog, predefined doc links per product (Graph, Pages, Instagram, WhatsApp, Messenger, Marketing), platform conventions — tokens, fields, pagination, errors, webhooks — and required-permissions answers with App Review awareness. Includes /meta-api:check and a Meta-keyword reminder hook. Meta platform only; defers general third-party doc verification to api-docs-first and designing your own APIs to api-design.

plugins.name: taskmaster
plugins.description: Idea-to-execution clarification: brainstorm fuzzy ideas into approved design docs, batched clarifying questions against an ambiguity ledger, consent-gated theme-aware shell mockups (full / ASCII-only / none, asked once per session) rendered in the project’s own colors when detectable, with compare modes, tradeoff callouts, and motion passes, on a single always-live auto-reload preview URL for visual decisions, interactive clickable walkthrough demos for whole experiences (multi-screen flows, states, failure exits), then a spec and milestone-grouped single-prompt task cards. Includes /taskmaster:task, /taskmaster:brainstorm, a context-scout agent, and a thin-feature-prompt reminder hook. Pairs with stack-scan, task-runner, and code-architecture for the full workflow (see README).

plugins.name: design-preview
plugins.description: Real-component visual decisions for Vite + React projects: renders 2-3 candidate variants with the project's OWN components on its own dev server via a scratch HTML entry (zero edits to existing files), behind a strict consent gate with guaranteed cleanup. Escalation tier above static shell mockups — falls back to them when Vite is absent. Includes /design-preview:preview.

plugins.name: git-workflow
plugins.description: Git workflow discipline: isolated worktrees for feature work, a structured finish protocol for development branches (verify, present options, merge/PR/park, clean up), and review-exchange rigor — what to include when requesting review, and technical verification before implementing received feedback. Includes /git-workflow:finish.

plugins.name: debugging
plugins.description: Systematic debugging: root cause before any fix — reproduce first, read the actual error, isolate with hypothesis tests and bisection, one change at a time, verify against the original symptom; escalation rule after three failed fixes. Includes /debugging:debug.

plugins.name: task-runner
plugins.description: Disciplined task execution: one task at a time, scope locked, bounded verify-fix inner loop (max three cycles, then park with evidence), no unbounded outer loop, full-suite completion gate; status lives in the task index, not HTML dashboards. Includes /task-runner:run and /task-runner:plan (computed subagents-vs-inline verdict: dependency levels, agent count, speedup estimate).

plugins.name: stack-scan
plugins.description: Inventory the actual stack from composer/npm/yarn/pnpm/bun manifests and lockfiles, runtime pins, and docker/CI images; required-vs-installed table with drift, missing-lock, and EOL flags. Includes /stack-scan:report.

plugins.name: plugin-scout
plugins.description: Scan the current project's manifests and suggest which cc-plugins-marketplace plugins to install — stack-matched tier and always-useful tier — then install the picked ones after confirmation. Includes /plugin-scout:suggest.

plugins.name: testing
plugins.description: Testing best practices: test pyramid and what to actually test, Pest/PHPUnit and Vitest/Jest idioms, Playwright/Dusk e2e discipline, factories and fixtures, mocking boundaries, flaky-test causes, coverage traps — plus the TDD workflow (red-green-refactor, red-green regression proof for bug fixes). Includes /testing:review and a test-engineer worker agent.

plugins.name: security
plugins.description: Security review for web apps: OWASP-aligned checks for injection, XSS, CSRF, authorization vs authentication, mass assignment, file uploads, secrets handling, and dependency audit — mapped to PHP/Laravel and JS/Vue specifics. Includes /security:review and a security-engineer worker agent.

plugins.name: typescript
plugins.description: TypeScript best practices: strict mode as the floor, any vs unknown discipline, narrowing over assertions, generics restraint, satisfies, runtime validation at boundaries, tsconfig hygiene. Includes /typescript:review.

plugins.name: inertia
plugins.description: Inertia.js best practices (Laravel + Vue/React/Svelte adapters): partial reloads, deferred and lazy props, useForm flow, shared data via middleware, prop hygiene, SSR, code splitting, v1 vs v2 and adapter awareness. Includes /inertia:review.

plugins.name: api-design
plugins.description: REST API design: resource naming, status-code discipline, pagination/filtering/sorting conventions, versioning strategy, RFC 9457 error format, idempotency, Laravel API Resources, and a live contract-preview artifact with real example payloads for new endpoints. Includes /api-design:review. For designing your own APIs; defers consuming third-party API docs to api-docs-first and Meta/Facebook platform specifics to meta-api.

plugins.name: dev-env
plugins.description: Dev environment initiator: scan composer/npm manifests, lockfiles, .env DSNs, and CI images, then generate a docker-compose.yml and Dockerfile matched to the actual stack — PHP version and extensions, Node, database engine, Redis, queues, mail. Proposes the topology as a diagram before writing YAML. Includes /dev-env:init to generate and /dev-env:review to audit existing docker files. Pairs with stack-scan. Owns local development environments; defers CI/CD pipelines and production deploys to the devops plugin.

plugins.name: web-dev
plugins.description: Generalist web development worker agent: routing, REST/API integration, forms and validation, state management, SSR/CSR trade-offs, baseline accessibility — implements, not just reviews. Stack-agnostic; defers to the per-framework review plugins. Includes a web-developer agent.

plugins.name: system-design
plugins.description: System-level design worker agent: service boundaries, data modeling, scaling paths, caching layers, sync vs async decisions — documents trade-offs while implementing. Complements code-architecture (code-level structure) without overlapping it. Includes a system-architect agent.

plugins.name: devops
plugins.description: DevOps worker agent: CI/CD pipeline design, Dockerfile and compose authoring, Kubernetes manifests, deploy strategies (blue-green, canary, rolling), observability wiring, secrets handling — implements pipeline and infra config end to end. Includes a devops-engineer agent. Owns CI/CD and production infrastructure; defers generating local docker-compose dev environments to the dev-env plugin.

plugins.name: database
plugins.description: Database worker agent: schema design, additive migrations, indexing strategy, query optimization, connection pooling — implements database changes engine-agnostically and defers dialect review to the sql/mysql/mariadb/postgresql plugins. Includes a database-engineer agent.

plugins.name: performance
plugins.description: Performance worker agent: measure-first profiling, bundle-size reduction, caching strategy, Core Web Vitals, N+1 elimination, load-test setup — optimizes only what a measurement proves slow. Includes a performance-engineer agent.

plugins.name: claude-authoring
plugins.description: Authoring guides for Claude Code artifacts — skills, agents, hooks, plugins — plus a routine-detector that suggests scaffolding a project skill when work turns repetitive, and /claude-authoring:new-skill|new-agent|new-hook|new-plugin scaffold commands.

plugins.name: code-review
plugins.description: Stack-agnostic code review: correctness bugs, code smells, convention drift on any diff or PR — severity-sorted one-line findings. Defers structure/YAGNI to code-architecture, security depth to security, stack idioms to the per-framework review plugins. Includes /code-review:review, a code-reviewer agent, and a code-smells skill.

plugins.name: approaches
plugins.description: Solution-approach deliberation: before non-trivial implementation, generate 2-3 structurally different approaches, compare trade-offs, pick with a stated kill-trigger — plus a strategy catalog (tracer bullet, walking skeleton, spike, strangler fig, inversion, Polya) mapped to the risk each one beats. Includes /approaches:compare.

plugins.name: decision-records
plugins.description: Architecture Decision Records: persist every significant technical choice — approach picks, schema shapes, dependency adoptions — to taskmaster-docs/adr/ with context, rejected options, consequences, and a revisit-when trigger. Status lifecycle proposed/accepted/superseded. Includes /decision-records:new.

plugins.name: retrospective
plugins.description: Post-milestone learning loop: five-minute retro after a task run or feature lands — surprises become CLAUDE.md candidates, repetition becomes skill suggestions, friction becomes process tweaks. Closes the amnesia gap where every run starts smart and ends forgotten. Includes /retrospective:run.

plugins.name: hindsight
plugins.description: Cross-session self-improvement loop: a SessionEnd hook records per-session friction stats (turns, errors, best-effort friction events) to a project-local ledger; /hindsight:harvest ranks high-friction sessions, deep-mines transcripts via a transcript-miner agent with a ≥2-session recurrence gate, and proposes CLAUDE.md rules, skill/plugin ideas, and failed-approach warnings — applied only on explicit approval. Includes /hindsight:harvest and a transcript-miner agent.

plugins.name: build-vs-buy
plugins.description: Gate zero before writing generic capability: check whether a library, service, or stdlib feature already solves it — candidate health table, take/wrap/write verdict, never-hand-roll list (crypto, auth, timezones, parsers). Includes /build-vs-buy:check.

plugins.name: rollout
plugins.description: Per-feature rollout planning: feature flags, backward compatibility, staged exposure (internal → percentage → full), migration sequencing (expand-migrate-contract), and a rollback path stated BEFORE ship. Includes /rollout:plan.

plugins.name: resilience
plugins.description: Failure-mode design for integration points: timeouts everywhere, retries with backoff and idempotency, circuit breaking, graceful degradation, backpressure, delivery semantics — designed in, not bolted on after the outage. Includes /resilience:review.

plugins.name: docs-upkeep
plugins.description: Documentation drift prevention: after any change, sync README claims, changelog entries, ADR links, and API docs with what the code now actually does — docs updated in the same change that invalidated them. Includes /docs-upkeep:check.

plugins.name: packages
plugins.description: Composer/npm dependency hygiene: semver constraint strategy, lockfile discipline, security-audit triage, upgrade lanes. Includes /packages:audit

plugins.name: orchestration
plugins.description: Subagent orchestration discipline: delegation contracts (self-contained prompts, compressed evidence-backed returns, model/effort tiering, scout-then-fanout, writer isolation) and verification panels (refuter voting, judge panels, loop-until-dry, completeness critic). Includes /orchestration:review.

plugins.name: estimation
plugins.description: Task sizing and effort estimation: S/M/L/XL classes with concrete anchors, reference-class calibration against work actually completed, uncertainty multipliers for unknowns, split triggers for oversized work, and estimate-vs-actual tracking that feeds retrospectives. Includes /estimation:size.

plugins.name: a11y
plugins.description: Dedicated accessibility audit: WCAG 2.1 AA checklist for markup, contrast, keyboard navigation, focus management, forms, media, and ARIA — one line per violation with the concrete fix. Includes /a11y:audit.

plugins.name: observability
plugins.description: Application observability with judgment: structured JSON logs with correlation IDs, log levels that mean something, RED/USE metrics without cardinality bombs, trace-context propagation, symptom-based alerting, honest health checks. Includes /observability:review.

plugins.name: error-handling
plugins.description: Language-agnostic error-handling discipline: crash on programmer errors, handle operational errors where you can act, typed errors over message-string matching, cause chains preserved across boundaries, one report per failure — no swallowed exceptions, no catch-log-continue towers. Includes /error-handling:review.

plugins.name: concurrency
plugins.description: Application-level concurrency safety: check-then-act races, optimistic vs pessimistic locking, idempotency keys for retried operations, queue-consumer dedup under at-least-once delivery, distributed locks with TTL and fencing, async parallel-write pitfalls — safe when two copies run at once. Includes /concurrency:review.

plugins.name: frontend-suite
plugins.description: Meta-bundle: the frontend category in one install — UI/UX stacks, React, React Native, Vue 2/3, TypeScript, Inertia, Livewire, the generalist web worker, and accessibility auditing. Uninstall cleanly with /frontend-suite:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.

plugins.name: php-suite
plugins.description: Meta-bundle: the PHP category in one install — PHP and Laravel best practices, Livewire, Inertia, and the generalist web worker. Uninstall cleanly with /php-suite:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.

plugins.name: db-suite
plugins.description: Meta-bundle: the database category in one install — engine-agnostic SQL discipline, MySQL, MariaDB, PostgreSQL best practices, and the database worker agent. Uninstall cleanly with /db-suite:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.

plugins.name: quality-suite
plugins.description: Meta-bundle: the code-quality category in one install — review, architecture principles, design patterns, testing, security, accessibility, debugging, performance, resilience, dependency hygiene, observability, error handling, and concurrency safety. Uninstall cleanly with /quality-suite:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.

plugins.name: process-suite
plugins.description: Meta-bundle: the engineering-process category in one install — git workflow, approach deliberation, decision records, retrospectives, hindsight mining, build-vs-buy gates, rollout planning, docs upkeep, estimation, subagent orchestration, task execution, stack scanning, and plugin scouting. Uninstall cleanly with /process-suite:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.

plugins.name: everything
plugins.description: Meta-bundle: installs every plugin in this marketplace as a dependency — all stacks, all workflows, all agents. One install, full suite. Uninstall cleanly with /everything:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.

plugins.name: taskmaster-suite
plugins.description: Meta-bundle: the full clarification-to-execution workflow plus every stack-agnostic capability — taskmaster, task-runner, stack-scan, code-architecture, general engineering discipline, UI/UX, and all worker agents. No framework- or dialect-specific plugins. Uninstall cleanly with /taskmaster-suite:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.
